# komodo_repo (Data Source)

Reads an existing Komodo git repository.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "komodo_repo" "example" {
  name = "my-repo"
}

output "repo_branch" {
  value = data.komodo_repo.example.source.branch
}
```

## Argument Reference

- `name` (Required) - The name of the git repository.

## Attributes Reference

- `id` - The git repository identifier (ObjectId).
- `server_id` - The ID of the server the repo is cloned on.
- `builder_id` - The ID of the attached builder.
- `path` - The folder on the server the repo is cloned into.
- `links` - Quick links associated with this repository.
- `source` - Git provider configuration. See [source](#nestedatt--source) below.
- `webhook` - Webhook configuration. See [webhook](#nestedatt--webhook) below.
- `on_clone` - The command run after the repository is cloned. See [on_clone / on_pull](#nestedatt--on_clone) below.
- `on_pull` - The command run after the repository is pulled. See [on_clone / on_pull](#nestedatt--on_pull) below.
- `environment` - Environment variable configuration. See [environment](#nestedatt--environment) below.

<a id="nestedatt--source"></a>
### Nested Schema for `source`

- `url` - The URL of the git provider, e.g. `https://github.com`.
- `account_id` - The git account used for private repositories.
- `path` - The repository path, e.g. `owner/repo`.
- `branch` - The branch checked out.
- `commit` - The specific commit hash checked out.

<a id="nestedatt--webhook"></a>
### Nested Schema for `webhook`

- `enabled` - Whether webhooks trigger an action on this repository.
- `secret` - The alternate webhook secret.

<a id="nestedatt--on_clone"></a>
<a id="nestedatt--on_pull"></a>
### Nested Schema for `on_clone` / `on_pull`

- `path` - The working directory for the command.
- `command` - The shell command to run.

<a id="nestedatt--environment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `environment`

- `file_path` - Path to the environment file.
- `variables` - Map of environment variables injected. Keys are uppercased.
